channel.title: 2026-04-10 AI × Science 文献日报 RSS
channel.description: 2026-04-10 当天筛选出的 AI × 物理 / 化学 / 材料交叉文献 RSS。

item.title: Equivariant Many-body Message Passing Interatomic Potentials for Magnetic Materials
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Cheuk Hin Ho, Cas van der Oord, James P. Darby, Theo Keane, Raz L. Benson, Cristian Rebolledo Espinoza, Rutvij Kulkarni, Elina Spinu, Michail Papanikolaou, Richard Tomsett, Robert M. Forrest, Jonathan J. Bean, Gábor Csányi, Christoph Ortner
arXiv:2604.08143v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Magnetism governs key properties of materials used in energy, data storage, and spintronic technologies, yet its complex coupling to lattice and electronic degrees of freedom challenges conventional first-principles approaches. We introduce an equivariant message-passing graph neural network that embeds atomic magnetic moments as explicit degrees of freedom, enabling the learning of magnetic interactions beyond collinear approximations. The model learns physically consistent and transferable representations of magnetic behaviour and can incorporate spin-orbit coupling, achieving near density-functional-theory accuracy with strong data efficiency across diverse magnetic systems by fine-tuning from a pre-trained model. Applications to structur…

item.title: Quantum-impurity sensing of altermagnetic order
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 V. A. S. V. Bittencourt, Hossein Hosseinabadi, Jairo Sinova, Libor Šmejkal, Jamir Marino
arXiv:2508.04788v3 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Quantum sensing with individual spin defects has emerged as a versatile platform to probe microscopic properties of condensed matter systems. Here we demonstrate that quantum relaxometry with nitrogen-vacancy (NV) centers in diamond can reveal the anisotropic spin dynamics of altermagnetic insulators together with their characteristic spin polarised bands. We show that the distance and orientation dependent relaxation rate of a nearby quantum impurity encodes signatures of momentum space anisotropy in the spin diffusion response, a hallmark of altermagnetic order. This directional sensitivity is unprecedented in the landscape of quantum materials sensing, and it enables the distinction of altermagnets from conventional antiferromagnets v…

item.title: Symmetry-guided and AI-accelerated design of intercalated transition metal dichalcogenides for antiferromagnetic spintronics
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Yu Pang, Yue Gu, Runsheng Zhong, Liyang Zou, Xiaobin Chen, Xiaolong Zou, Wenhui Duan
arXiv:2604.07689v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The advancement of antiferromagnetic spintronics depends on quantum materials with target symmetry-dictated functionalities, however, their systematic discovery is hindered by the immense configurational complexity of the available material space. Here, we introduce a symmetry-guided, AI-accelerated framework incorporating graph neural networks with high generalization ability to overcome this bottleneck. Based on fully intercalated transition metal dichalcogenides (iTMDs) and using only 200 relaxed partially intercalated structures for transfer learning, our model effectively explores more than 100,000 partially intercalated configurations and identifies 35 altermagnetic and 20 Tτ-antiferromagnetic ground-state candidates. Interestingly, we…

item.title: 磁学中的机器学习：进展、挑战与展望
item.description:
📚 ScienceDirect
该工作系统综述机器学习在磁学及磁性材料研究中的应用现状。涵盖磁性结构预测、磁相互作用建模、居里温度估算等方向，分析当前面临的关键挑战并展望未来发展路径，详情需查阅原文。

item.title: Closing the Loop in Epitaxy with Machine Learning: Joint Optimization of Growth and Geometry in On-Chip Lasers
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Mihir R. Athavale, Stephen A. Church, Wei Wen Wong, Andre KY Low, Hark Hoe Tan, Kedar Hippalgaonkar, Patrick Parkinson
arXiv:2604.08390v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Achieving device-to-device reproducibility is a critical bottleneck for scalable photonic integrated circuits, as subtle variations in bottom-up epitaxial growth and fabrication severely limit yield. We present a machine learning workflow for III-V multi-quantum well microring lasers that first optimizes growth and geometry parameters via multi-objective Bayesian optimization, then leverages variational autoencoders (VAEs) to attribute residual device-to-device variability to its underlying sources. By explicitly targeting threshold variance alongside absolute performance, we demonstrate 100% lasing yield across all designs. The optimized multi-quantum well microring laser fields achieved a median lasing threshold of 16~μJ,cm⁻²,pulse⁻¹, a 73…

item.title: DustNET: enabling machine learning and AI models of dusty plasmas
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Zhehui Wang, Justin C. Burton, Niklas Dormagen, Cheng-Ran Du, Yan Feng, John E. Foster, Susan S. Glenn, Max Klein, Christina A. Knapek, Lorin Matthews, André Melzer, Edward Thomas, Chuji Wang, Jalaan Avritte, Shan Chang, Neeraj Chaubey, Pubuduni Ekanayaka, John A. Goree, Truell Hyde, Chen Liang, Zhuang Liu, Zhuang Ma, Ilya Nemenman, Elon Price, A. S. Schmitz, Saikat C. Thakur, M. H. Thoma, Hubertus Thomas, L. Wimmer, Wei Yang, Zimu Yang, Xiaoman Zhang
arXiv:2603.17493v3 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Dusty plasmas are ubiquitous throughout the universe, spanning laboratory and industrial plasmas, fusion devices, planetary environments, cometary comae, and interstellar media. Despite decades of research, many aspects of their behavior remain poorly understood within a unified framework. While numerous theoretical and numerical models describe specific phenomena, such as dust charging, transport, waves, and self-organization, fully predictive models across the wide range of spatial and temporal scales in both laboratory and natural systems remain elusive. Conventional plasma descriptions rely on coupled differential equations for particle densities, momenta, and energies, but their solutions are often limited by computational cost, num…

item.title: Evaluating PQC KEMs, Combiners, and Cascade Encryption via Adaptive IND-CPA Testing Using Deep Learning
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Simon Calderon, Niklas Johansson, Onur Günlü
arXiv:2604.06942v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Ensuring ciphertext indistinguishability is fundamental to cryptographic security, but empirically validating this property in real implementations and hybrid settings presents practical challenges. The transition to post-quantum cryptography (PQC), with its hybrid constructions combining classical and quantum-resistant primitives, makes empirical validation approaches increasingly valuable. By modeling IND-CPA games as binary classification tasks and training on labeled ciphertext data with BCE loss, we study deep neural network (DNN) distinguishers for ciphertext indistinguishability. We apply this methodology to PQC KEMs. We specifically test the public-key encryption (PKE) schemes used to construct examples such as ML-KEM, BIKE, and HQ…

item.title: Group Convolutional Neural Network for the Low-Energy Spectrum in the Quantum Dimer Model
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Ojasvi Sharma, Sandipan Manna, Prashant Shekhar Rao, G J Sreejith
arXiv:2505.23728v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: We obtain the m̊p4m-symmetric Group Convolutional Neural Network (GCNN) representations of the lowest energy eigenstate of the quantum dimer model on L× L square-lattice in each of the (L²⁺¹⁸L+72)/8 irreducible representations (irreps) of the lattice space group and use these to investigate the competition between columnar, plaquette and mixed phases. The networks are optimized within each irrep by minimizing the energy, which is estimated from samples obtained via a directed loop sampler. In extensive benchmarks, we show excellent agreement in energy estimates, order parameters and correlation functions with exact diagonalization or quantum Monte Carlo in systems of sizes 8łeq Lłeq 32. Analysis of the scaling of the gaps in different re…

item.title: Machine Learning the order-disorder Jahn-Teller transition in LaMnO₃
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Lorenzo Celiberti, Alexander Ehrentraut, Luca Leoni, Cesare Franchini
arXiv:2604.08058v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We investigate the Jahn-Teller structural phase transition in LaMnO₃ at TJT simeq 750 K using molecular dynamics simulations based on machine-learning force fields trained on ab initio data. Analysis of the site-site correlation function of the distortions reveals that the transition is driven by the ordering of the Q₂ Jahn-Teller distortion of the MnO₆ octahedra, which acts as the order parameter and establishes the order-disorder nature of the transition. Dynamical local distortions are found to persist above TJT. Our results reproduce the experimental temperature dependence of both structural and phonon properties and highlight the presence of anharmonic effects at finite temperature. More broadly, the combined use of machine-learning mol…

item.title: Neural parametric representations for thin-shell shape optimisation
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Xiao Xiao, Fehmi Cirak
arXiv:2604.06612v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Shape optimisation of thin-shell structures requires a flexible, differentiable geometric representation suitable for gradient-based optimisation. We propose a neural parametric representation (NRep) for the shell mid-surface based on a neural network with periodic activation functions. The NRep is defined using a multi-layer perceptron (MLP), which maps the parametric coordinates of mid-surface vertices to their physical coordinates. A structural compliance optimisation problem is posed to optimise the shape of a thin-shell parameterised by the NRep subject to a volume constraint, with the network parameters as design variables. The resulting shape optimisation problem is solved using a gradient-based optimisation algorithm. Benchmark exa…

item.title: QNAS: A Neural Architecture Search Framework for Accurate and Efficient Quantum Neural Networks
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Kooshan Maleki, Alberto Marchisio, Muhammad Shafique
arXiv:2604.07013v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Designing quantum neural networks (QNNs) that are both accurate and deployable on NISQ hardware is challenging. Handcrafted ansatze must balance expressivity, trainability, and resource use, while limited qubits often necessitate circuit cutting. Existing quantum architecture search methods primarily optimize accuracy while only heuristically controlling quantum and mostly ignore the exponential overhead of circuit cutting. We introduce QNAS, a neural architecture search framework that unifies hardware aware evaluation, multi objective optimization, and cutting overhead awareness for hybrid quantum classical neural networks (HQNNs). QNAS trains a shared parameter SuperCircuit and uses NSGA-II to optimize three objectives jointly: (i) valid…

item.title: SMT-AD: a scalable quantum-inspired anomaly detection approach
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Apimuk Sornsaeng, Si Min Chan, Wenxuan Zhang, Swee Liang Wong, Joshua Lim, Dario Poletti
arXiv:2604.06265v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Quantum-inspired tensor networks algorithms have shown to be effective and efficient models for machine learning tasks, including anomaly detection. Here, we propose a highly parallelizable quantum-inspired approach which we call SMT-AD from Superposition of Multiresolution Tensors for Anomaly Detection. It is based upon the superposition of bond-dimension-1 matrix product operators to transform the input data with Fourier-assisted feature embedding, where the number of learnable parameters grows linearly with feature size, embedding resolutions, and the number of additional components in the matrix product operators structure. We demonstrate successful anomaly detection when applied to standard datasets, including credit card transactions,…

item.title: Soft-Quantum Algorithms
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Basil Kyriacou, Mo Kordzanganeh, Maniraman Periyasamy, Alexey Melnikov
arXiv:2604.06523v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Quantum operations on pure states can be fully represented by unitary matrices. Variational quantum circuits, also known as quantum neural networks, embed data and trainable parameters into gate-based operations and optimize the parameters via gradient descent. The high cost of training and low fidelity of current quantum devices, however, restricts much of quantum machine learning to classical simulation. For few-qubit problems with large datasets, training the matrix elements directly, as is done with weight matrices in classical neural networks, can be faster than decomposing data and parameters into gates. We propose a method that trains matrices directly while maintaining unitarity through a single regularization term added to the los…

item.title: Temperature-dependent Raman spectra of 2H-MoS2 from Machine Learning-driven statistical sampling
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Samuel Longo, Aloïs Castellano, Matthieu J. Verstraete
arXiv:2604.02538v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Molybdenum sulfides are in the spotlight of materials science thanks to their interesting properties for applications in optoelectronics, nanocomposites, lubricants, and catalysis. The structural characterization of Molybdenum sulfides is a crucial step to understand and tune their properties. Vibrational techniques, such as infrared and Raman spectroscopy, can directly link to structural features, but the experimental literature suffers from large variability. Theoretical calculations are a powerful tool complementing and explaining empirical measurements. The reliability of first-principles calculation depends on the level of approximation made, taking into account disorder, doping, or temperature to yield a good description of the pho…

item.title: Tensor-Augmented Convolutional Neural Networks: Enhancing Expressivity with Generic Tensor Kernels
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Chia-Wei Hsing, Wei-Lin Tu
arXiv:2604.08072v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) excel at extracting local features hierarchically, but their performance in capturing complex correlations hinges heavily on deep architectures, which are usually computationally demanding and difficult to interpret. To address these issues, we propose a physically-guided shallow model: tensor-augmented CNN (TACNN), which replaces conventional convolution kernels with generic tensors to enhance representational capacity. This choice is motivated by the fact that an order-N tensor naturally encodes an arbitrary quantum superposition state in the Hilbert space of dimension d^N, where d is the local physical dimension, thus offering substantially richer expressivity. Furthermore, in our design the convolut…

item.title: Classical fracton spin liquid and Hilbert space fragmentation in a 2D spin-1/2 model
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Nils Niggemann, Meghadeepa Adhikary, Yannik Schaden-Thillmann, and Johannes Reuther
Author(s): Nils Niggemann, Meghadeepa Adhikary, Yannik Schaden-Thillmann, and Johannes Reuther Classical U(1) fracton spin liquids feature an extensive ground state degeneracy and follow an effective description in terms of a tensor Gauss's law where charges, so-called fractons, have restricted mobility. Here we introduce a simple spin model that realizes such a state by straightforward discr… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 165118] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026

item.title: Diagonal isometric form for tensor network states in two dimensions
item.description:
📚 Editors' Suggestions
👤 Benjamin Sappler, Masataka Kawano, Michael P. Zaletel, and Frank Pollmann
Author(s): Benjamin Sappler, Masataka Kawano, Michael P. Zaletel, and Frank Pollmann Isometric tensor network states (isoTNS) generalize the isometric form of the one-dimensional matrix product states (MPS) to tensor networks in two and higher dimensions. Here, the authors introduce an alternative isometric form for isoTNS by incorporating auxiliary tensors to represent the orthogonality hypersurface. The authors demonstrate the viability of the method by performing ground-state search and real-time evolution of the transverse field Ising model on large square lattices of up to 1250 sites. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 165117] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026

item.title: Na₂K体系神经网络势能面及Na+NaK→Na₂+K反应的量子动力学研究
item.description:
📚 ScienceDirect
该工作构建了Na₂K三原子体系的神经网络势能面，并基于此开展该反应的量子散射动力学计算，以高精度势能面替代昂贵电子结构计算，实现低温条件下反应截面和速率常数的精确预测。

item.title: 混合量子-经典机器学习的实用价值：VQE与QAOA在NISQ时代模拟器上的严格基准测试
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Shahriar Ahsan Taisiq, Tahera Shafina Toma
本研究对两种旗舰混合量子-经典算法——用于分类的变分量子本征求解器(VQE)和用于组合优化的量子近似优化算法(QAOA)——进行了全面的实证评估，并与同等资源约束下的先进经典基线模型对比。模拟结果显示，尽管量子方法取得一定竞争力（如VQE二分类准确率91.2%），但始终落后于优化后的经典模型（如支持向量机94.8%），且计算时间高出数个数量级。研究还实证验证了噪声敏感性、贫瘠高原等关键可扩展性障碍。

item.title: 结合机器学习与DFT从电子结构预测难熔高熵合金的力学性能
item.description:
📚 ScienceDirect
该工作融合密度泛函理论(DFT)电子结构计算与机器学习方法，建立难熔高熵合金电子特征与其力学性能（如硬度、弹性模量等）的关联模型，实现面向高温结构应用的高熵合金快速筛选与性能预测。详情需查阅原文。

item.title: 利用机器学习预测模型铜氧化物超导体的参数
item.description:
📚 ScienceDirect
该工作将机器学习方法应用于典型铜氧化物高温超导体的物理参数预测。通过数据驱动建模建立材料微观参数与宏观超导特性之间的关联，为理解高温超导机理及材料设计提供新途径，详情需查阅原文。

item.title: 基于机器学习驱动原子模拟的金刚石及硬碳同素异形体热输运性质
item.description:
📚 ScienceDirect
该工作采用机器学习势结合原子模拟方法研究金刚石及新型硬碳同素异形体的晶格热导率。揭示微观结构、成键特征与声子输运机制对热输运性能的影响规律，为超硬材料的热管理应用提供理论指导，详情需查阅原文。

item.title: La掺杂调控热障陶瓷SrZrO₃热导率：基于机器学习势的计算研究
item.description:
📚 ScienceDirect
该工作利用机器学习原子间势开展大规模分子动力学模拟，研究镧掺杂对钙钛矿结构热障陶瓷SrZrO₃晶格热导率的影响机制，探索通过点缺陷工程降低热导率、提升热障涂层隔热性能的可行途径。详情需查阅原文。

item.title: 基于机器学习原子间势理解无机固体电解质界面膜组分LiF和Li₂O的热导率
item.description:
📚 ScienceDirect
该工作开发机器学习原子间势模型，用于精确模拟锂离子电池固体电解质界面膜(SEI)关键无机组分LiF和Li₂O的晶格热输运行为，揭示其微观热导机制，为优化电池热管理策略提供原子尺度的理论认识。详情需查阅原文。

item.title: Comparing fine-tuning strategies of MACE machine learning force field for modeling Li-ion diffusion in LiF for batteries
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Nada Alghamdi, Paolo de Angelis, Pietro Asinari, Eliodoro Chiavazzo
arXiv:2510.05020v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Machine learning force fields (MLFFs) are transforming materials science and engineering by enabling the study of complex phenomena, such as those critical to battery operation. In this work, we explore the predictive capabilities of pre-trained and fine-tuned MACE MLFF and compare different fine-tuning strategies for predicting interstitial lithium diffusivity in LiF, a key component in the solid electrolyte interphase in Li-ion batteries. Our results demonstrate that the MACE-MPA-0 foundational model achieves comparable accuracy to well-trained DeePMD, in predicting key diffusion properties based on large scale molecular dynamics simulation, while requiring minimal or no training data. For instance, the MACE-MPA-0 predicts an activatio…

item.title: 基于自监督物理信息神经网络学习恒星结构方程
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Manuel Ballester, Santiago Lopez-Tapia, Seth Gossage, Patrick Koller, Philipp M. Srivastava, Ugur Demir, Yongseok Jo, Almudena P. Marquez, Christoph Wuersch, Souvik Chakraborty, Vicky Kalogera, Aggelos Katsaggelos
针对传统自适应有限差分求解器（如MESA）在大规模恒星群体合成中计算昂贵、难以扩展的问题，本文提出自监督物理信息神经网络（PINN）框架，以无网格、全可微方式求解流体静力学与热平衡下的恒星结构方程。模型输入恒星边界条件与化学组成，通过物理损失项学习质量、压强、密度、温度与光度的连续径向分布；并引入辅助神经网络将状态方程与不透明度表近似为局部热力学状态的光滑可微函数，以纳入真实微观物理。

item.title: [ASAP] Accelerating Multi-Elemental Catalyst Discovery with Interpretable Machine Learning and Automated Experimentation
item.description:
📚 ACS Nano: Latest Articles (ACS Publications)
👤 Fangzheng Liu, Zhilong Chen, Han Hu, Cunjin Li, Lisong Zhang, Zeming Liu, and Guangxu Chen
ACS Nano DOI: 10.1021/acsnano.5c20552

item.title: Deciphering the Mechanism and Activity of Diatomic Catalysts for Urea Electrosynthesis with DFT and Machine Learning
item.description:
📚 Wiley
👤 Li Shi, Xiaobing Wang, Wenqian Yang, Xin Wang, Xiuyun Zhang, Yanwen Ma, Jin Zhao
Advanced Functional Materials, Volume 36, Issue 29, 9 April 2026.

item.title: 通过神经网络替代CALPHAD计算加速MOX燃料热化学建模：集成至SCIANTIX
item.description:
📚 ScienceDirect
该工作致力于开发基于神经网络的CALPHAD热力学计算替代模型，以加速混合氧化物(MOX)核燃料的热化学模拟，并将其集成至SCIANTIX燃料性能分析框架中，旨在突破传统热力学计算在核燃料多尺度模拟中的效率瓶颈。详情需查阅原文。

item.title: DFT与机器学习联合解析双原子催化剂电合成尿素的机理与活性
item.description:
📚 Wiley
👤 Li Shi, Xiaobing Wang, Wenqian Yang, Xin Wang, Xiuyun Zhang, Yanwen Ma, Jin Zhao
该工作结合密度泛函理论（DFT）计算与机器学习方法，系统解析双原子催化剂电催化合成尿素的反应机理并预测催化活性。研究为高效尿素电合成催化剂的设计提供理论指导，详情需查阅原文。

item.title: 选择性差分注意力增强的笛卡尔原子矩机器学习原子间势及其跨体系迁移性
item.description:
📚 ScienceDirect
该工作提出选择性差分注意力机制改进笛卡尔原子矩描述符，构建具有跨体系迁移能力的机器学习原子间势。通过注意力加权优化局域环境表征，提升势函数在不同化学体系间的泛化性能，详情需查阅原文。

item.title: Calibration of a neural network ocean closure for improved mean state and variability
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Pavel Perezhogin, Alistair Adcroft, Laure Zanna
arXiv:2604.06398v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Global ocean models exhibit biases in the mean state and variability, particularly at coarse resolution, where mesoscale eddies are unresolved. To address these biases, parameterization coefficients are typically tuned ad hoc. Here, we formulate parameter tuning as a calibration problem using Ensemble Kalman Inversion (EKI). We optimize parameters of a neural network parameterization of mesoscale eddies in two idealized ocean models at coarse resolution. The calibrated parameterization reduces errors in the time-averaged fluid interfaces and their variability by approximately a factor of two compared to the unparameterized model or the offline-trained parameterization. The EKI method is robust to noise in time-averaged statistics arising f…

item.title: Comparing Ocean Forecasts Driven with Machine Learning-based and Physics-based Atmospheric Forcings
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Xiaobing Zhou, Frank Colberg, Debra Hudson, Yonghong Yin, Griffith Young, Christopher Bladwell, Catherine Deburgh-Day
arXiv:2604.07861v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Operational ocean forecasting systems conventionally employ dynamical ocean models driven by atmospheric forcing derived from numerical weather prediction (NWP) models. Recent advancements in artificial intelligence and machine learning (ML) have led to the development of ML-based atmospheric weather models, which have competitive, if not better, medium range forecast accuracy compared to traditional NWP systems. This study evaluates the impact of ML-based atmospheric forcing on ocean forecast skill through two sets of 10-day forecasts using the UK Met Office GOSI9 configuration of the NEMO dynamical ocean model. Both experiments share identical ocean initial conditions; but differ in atmospheric forcing: one uses ECMWF's ML-based AIFS model…

item.title: Hard-constrained Physics-informed Neural Networks for Interface Problems
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Seung Whan Chung, Stephen Castonguay, Sumanta Roy, Michael Penwarden, Yucheng Fu, Pratanu Roy
arXiv:2604.08453v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) have emerged as a flexible framework for solving partial differential equations, but their performance on interface problems remains challenging because continuity and flux conditions are typically imposed through soft penalty terms. The standard soft-constraint formulation leads to imperfect interface enforcement and degraded accuracy near interfaces. We introduce two ansatz-based hard-constrained PINN formulations for interface problems that embed the interface physics into the solution representation and thereby decouple interface enforcement from PDE residual minimization. The first, termed the windowing approach, constructs the trial space from compactly supported windowed subnetworks so that i…

item.title: Learning relaxation time distributions from spectral induced polarization data with a complex-valued variational autoencoder
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Charles L. Bérubé, Sébastien Gagnon, Lahiru M. A. Nagasingha, Jean-Luc Gagnon, E. Rachel Kenko, Reza Ghanati, Frédérique Baron
arXiv:2603.13973v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Spectral induced polarization (SIP) is a geophysical method used to characterize subsurface materials. It measures the frequency-dependent complex resistivity of rocks and soils through the application of a small alternating current in the subsurface or in laboratory samples. Debye decomposition (DD) is a standard method for analyzing and interpreting SIP data, as it allows estimation of the relaxation time distribution (RTD) of geomaterials. However, conventional DD approaches treat measurements independently, work in real-valued spaces despite the complex-valued nature of SIP data, and provide limited uncertainty quantification. These limitations reduce the effectiveness of conventional DD on heterogeneous datasets. We reformulate DD a…

item.title: Operator Learning for Surrogate Modeling of Wave-Induced Forces from Sea Surface Waves
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Shukai Cai, Sourav Dutta, Mark Loveland, Eirik Valseth, Peter Rivera-Casillas, Corey Trahan, Clint Dawson
arXiv:2604.06433v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Wave setup plays a significant role in transferring wave-induced energy to currents and causing an increase in water elevation. This excess momentum flux, known as radiation stress, motivates the coupling of circulation models with wave models to improve the accuracy of storm surge prediction, however, traditional numerical wave models are complex and computationally expensive. As a result, in practical coupled simulations, wave models are often executed at much coarser temporal resolution than circulation models. In this work, we explore the use of Deep Operator Networks (DeepONets) as a surrogate for the Simulating WAves Nearshore (SWAN) numerical wave model. The proposed surrogate model was tested on three distinct 1-D and 2-D steady-st…

item.title: Parameter-Efficient Transfer Learning for Microseismic Phase Picking Using a Neural Operator
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Ayrat Abdullin, Umair Bin Waheed, Leo Eisner, Naveed Iqbal
arXiv:2512.13197v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Seismic phase picking is fundamental for microseismic monitoring and subsurface imaging. Manual processing is impractical for real-time applications and large sensor arrays, motivating the use of deep learning-based pickers trained on extensive earthquake catalogs. On a broader scale, these models are generally tuned to perform optimally in high signal-to-noise and long-duration networks and often fail to perform satisfactorily when applied to campaign-based microseismic datasets, which are characterized by low signal-to-noise ratios, sparse geometries, and limited labeled data. In this study, we present a microseismic adaptation of a network-wide earthquake phase picker, Phase Neural Operator (PhaseNO), using transfer learning and param…

item.title: Physics-informed neural operators for the in situ characterization of locally reacting sound absorbers
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Jonas M. Schmid, Johannes D. Schmid, Martin Eser, Steffen Marburg
arXiv:2604.07412v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Accurate knowledge of acoustic surface admittance or impedance is essential for reliable wave-based simulations, yet its in situ estimation remains challenging due to noise, model inaccuracies, and restrictive assumptions of conventional methods. This work presents a physics-informed neural operator approach for estimating frequency-dependent surface admittance directly from near-field measurements of sound pressure and particle velocity. A deep operator network is employed to learn the mapping from measurement data, spatial coordinates, and frequency to acoustic field quantities, while simultaneously inferring a globally consistent surface admittance spectrum without requiring an explicit forward model. The governing acoustic relations, i…

item.title: Small-scale photonic Kolmogorov-Arnold networks using standard telecom nonlinear modules
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Luca Nogueira Calçado, Sergei K. Turitsyn, Egor Manuylovich
arXiv:2604.08432v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Photonic neural networks promise ultrafast inference, yet most architectures rely on linear optical meshes with electronic nonlinearities, reintroducing optical-electrical-optical bottlenecks. Here we introduce small-scale photonic Kolmogorov-Arnold networks (SSP-KANs) implemented entirely with standard telecommunications components. Each network edge employs a trainable nonlinear module composed of a Mach-Zehnder interferometer, semiconductor optical amplifier, and variable optical attenuators, providing a four-parameter transfer function derived from gain saturation and interferometric mixing. Despite this constrained expressivity, SSP-KANs comprising only a few optical modules achieve strong nonlinear inference performance across classifi…

item.title: A domain-specific machine learning potential model for metallic materials spanning 53 elements
item.description:
📚 npj Computational Materials
👤 Xin-Yang Li, Jing Li, Yi-Nan Wang, Na-Min Xiao, Wen-Yue Zhao, Zhang-Zhi Shi 等9位作者
npj Computational Materials, Published online: 10 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41524-026-02072-3 A domain-specific machine learning potential model for metallic materials spanning 53 elements

item.title: 基于深度学习的背散射电子图像晶界检测方法
item.description:
📚 ScienceDirect
该工作发展深度学习模型实现背散射电子（BSE）显微图像中晶界的自动识别与分割。解决传统图像处理方法对复杂衬度晶界检测的局限，为材料微观组织定量表征提供高效智能化工具，详情需查阅原文。

item.title: 基于机器学习势的GeSe₂玻璃热处理回春原子模拟
item.description:
📚 ScienceDirect
该工作围绕非晶态GeSe₂玻璃的热处理回春机制展开原子尺度模拟研究，采用机器学习势函数描述原子间相互作用。通过热过程模拟揭示玻璃结构恢复与性能调控的微观机理，为硫系玻璃材料的设计与优化提供理论依据，详情需查阅原文。

item.title: 基于多特征晶体图卷积神经网络的钙钛矿带隙预测
item.description:
📚 ScienceDirect
该工作围绕钙钛矿材料的带隙预测展开，采用多特征晶体图卷积神经网络(CGCNN)框架整合晶体结构信息，旨在建立高效准确的带隙预测模型，为钙钛矿光电器件的高通量筛选与设计提供计算工具。详情需查阅原文。

item.title: 基于物理信息神经网络的碳纳米管森林生长动力学与力学预测
item.description:
📚 ScienceDirect
该工作利用物理信息神经网络（PINNs）预测碳纳米管森林的生长动力学行为与力学性能，将物理约束嵌入深度学习框架以实现数据驱动与机理模型的融合，详情需查阅原文。

item.title: 用于数据驱动缺陷检测与表征的无监督机器学习流程：位移级联应用
item.description:
📚 ScienceDirect
该工作构建无监督机器学习流程实现辐照损伤位移级联中缺陷的自动识别与表征。绕过传统监督学习对标注数据的依赖，从大规模原子模拟数据中提取缺陷结构特征，为核材料辐照损伤分析提供高效方法，详情需查阅原文。

item.title: A Helicity-Conservative Domain-Decomposed Physics-Informed Neural Network for Incompressible Non-Newtonian Flow
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Zheng Lu, Young Ju Lee, Jiwei Jia, Ziqian Li
arXiv:2604.08002v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper develops a helicity-aware physics-informed neural network framework for incompressible non-Newtonian flow in rotational form. In addition to the energy law and the incompressibility constraint, helicity is a fundamental geometric quantity that characterizes the topology of vortex lines and plays an important role in the physical fidelity of long-time flow simulations. While helicity-preserving discretizations have been studied extensively in finite difference, finite element, and other structure-preserving settings, their realization within neural network solvers remains largely unexplored. Motivated by this gap, we propose a neural formulation in which vorticity is computed directly from the neural velocity field by automatic dif…

item.title: 贝叶斯神经网络中随机梯度朗之万动力学的自适应步长策略
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Rajit Rajpal, Benedict Leimkuhler, Yuanhao Jiang
针对贝叶斯神经网络后验采样中随机梯度MCMC方法对步长敏感的问题，本文基于SamAdams时间步自适应框架提出SA-SGLD算法。该方法通过时间重缩放机制，依据局部梯度范数动态调节步长，在高曲率区域自动缩小步长、平坦区域扩大步长，无需引入发散修正项即可兼顾稳定性与混合效率。实验表明，该方法在高曲率二维示例及采用尖锐先验的图像分类BNN任务中均优于标准SGLD。

item.title: Graph Neural ODE Digital Twins for Control-Oriented Reactor Thermal-Hydraulic Forecasting Under Partial Observability
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Akzhol Almukhametov, Doyeong Lim, Rui Hu, Yang Liu
arXiv:2604.07292v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Real-time supervisory control of advanced reactors requires accurate forecasting of plant-wide thermal-hydraulic states, including locations where physical sensors are unavailable. Meeting this need calls for surrogate models that combine predictive fidelity, millisecond-scale inference, and robustness to partial observability. In this work, we present a physics-informed message-passing Graph Neural Network coupled with a Neural Ordinary Differential Equation (GNN-ODE) to addresses all three requirements simultaneously. We represent the whole system as a directed sensor graph whose edges encode hydraulic connectivity through flow/heat transfer-aware message passing, and we advance the latent dynamics in continuous time via a controlled Neura…

item.title: 物理信息神经网络用于平流-扩散方程的联合源与参数估计
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Brenda Anague, Bamdad Hosseini, Issa Karambal, Jean Medard Ngnotchouye
本研究针对平流-扩散方程中源函数与PDE参数联合反演的严重不适定问题，提出了一种基于多网络物理信息神经网络神经正切核的加权自适应方法。该方法同时求解解场、未知源项及PDE参数的联合恢复，利用偏微分方程约束耦合多个未知函数，扩展了NTK在涉及多神经网络的反问题中的应用。

item.title: 弹性血管血流积分守恒物理信息神经网络的流固耦合训练
item.description:
📚 ScienceDirect
该工作提出一种基于流固耦合训练的积分守恒物理信息神经网络（IC-PINN）方法，用于模拟弹性血管中的血液流动问题，将流体力学与结构力学耦合嵌入神经网络损失函数以提升预测物理一致性。

item.title: A Practical Introduction to Tensor Network Renormalization with TNRKit.jl
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Victor Vanthilt, Adwait Naravane, Chenqi Meng, Atsushi Ueda
arXiv:2604.06922v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: We present TNRKit, an open-source Julia package for Tensor Network Renormalization (TNR) of two- and three-dimensional classical statistical models and Euclidean lattice field theories. Built on top of TensorKit, it provides a symmetry-aware framework for constructing tensor-network representations of partition functions and coarse-graining them using methods such as TRG, HOTRG, and LoopTNR. Beyond thermodynamic quantities, the package enables the extraction of universal conformal data -- including scaling dimensions and the central charge -- directly from fixed-point tensors. TNRKit is designed with both usability and extensibility in mind, offering a practical platform for applying, benchmarking, and developing modern tensor renormaliz…

item.title: HQF-Net：面向遥感图像分割的混合量子经典多尺度融合网络
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Md Aminur Hossain, Ayush V. Patel, Siddhant Gole, Sanjay K. Singh, Biplab Banerjee
针对遥感语义分割需联合捕获精细空间细节与高层语义语境的挑战，本文提出HQF-Net混合量子经典多尺度融合网络。该方法通过可变形多尺度交叉注意力融合模块整合冻结DINOv3 ViT-L/16骨干的多尺度语义引导与定制U-Net架构，并引入量子增强跳跃连接（QSkip）及量子混合专家瓶颈（QMoE），在自适应路由机制中组合互补的局部、全局与方向量子电路。在三个遥感基准上的实验显示一致性能提升，LandCover.ai上达0.8568 mIoU与96.87%总体精度。

item.title: Neural-quantum-states impurity solver for quantum embedding problems
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Zhanghao Zhouyin, Tsung-Han Lee, Ao Chen, Nicola Lanatà, and Hong Guo
Author(s): Zhanghao Zhouyin, Tsung-Han Lee, Ao Chen, Nicola Lanatà, and Hong Guo Neural quantum states (NQS) have emerged as a promising approach to solve second-quantized Hamiltonians, because of their scalability and flexibility. In this work, we design and benchmark an NQS impurity solver for the quantum embedding (QE) methods, focusing on the ghost Gutzwiller approximation (… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 155123] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026

item.title: 多GPU量子电路模拟及网络性能影响
item.description:
📚 ScienceDirect
该工作围绕多GPU架构下的量子电路模拟展开，重点考察网络性能对大规模量子计算模拟效率的影响，旨在优化分布式量子模拟的计算策略与通信开销。

item.title: Accelerating 4D Hyperspectral Imaging through Physics-Informed Neural Representation and Adaptive Sampling
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Chi-Jui Ho, Harsh Bhakta, Wei Xiong, Nicholas Antipa
arXiv:2604.06561v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: High-dimensional hyperspectral imaging (HSI) enables the visualization of ultrafast molecular dynamics and complex, heterogeneous spectra. However, applying this capability to resolve spatially varying vibrational couplings in two-dimensional infrared (2DIR) spectroscopy, a type of coherent multidimensional spectroscopy (CMDS), necessitates prohibitively long data acquisition, driven by dense Nyquist sampling requirements and the need for extensive signal accumulation. To address this challenge, we introduce a physics-informed neural representation approach that efficiently reconstructs dense spatially-resolved 2DIR hyperspectral images from sparse experimental measurements. In particular, we used a multilayer perceptron (MLP) to model the…

item.title: 四面体Fe₄团簇催化CO₂加氢甲烷化的反应网络DFT分析
item.description:
📚 ScienceDirect
该工作采用密度泛函理论（DFT）系统分析了四面体Fe₄团簇上CO₂甲烷化反应的完整反应网络，包括各基元反应路径、中间体稳定性及决速步，为理解铁基催化剂上的CO₂转化机理提供微观尺度见解。

item.title: Neural Computers
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Mingchen Zhuge, Changsheng Zhao, Haozhe Liu, Zijian Zhou, Shuming Liu, Wenyi Wang, Ernie Chang, Gael Le Lan, Junjie Fei, Wenxuan Zhang, Yasheng Sun, Zhipeng Cai, Zechun Liu, Yunyang Xiong, Yining Yang, Yuandong Tian, Yangyang Shi, Vikas Chandra, Jürgen Schmidhuber
arXiv:2604.06425v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We propose a new frontier: Neural Computers (NCs) -- an emerging machine form that unifies computation, memory, and I/O in a learned runtime state. Unlike conventional computers, which execute explicit programs, agents, which act over external execution environments, and world models, which learn environment dynamics, NCs aim to make the model itself the running computer. Our long-term goal is the Completely Neural Computer (CNC): the mature, general-purpose realization of this emerging machine form, with stable execution, explicit reprogramming, and durable capability reuse. As an initial step, we study whether early NC primitives can be learned solely from collected I/O traces, without instrumented program state. Concretely, we instantiate…

item.title: A battery-free wireless epidermal sensor network for continuous systolic blood pressure monitoring
item.description:
📚 Nature Electronics
👤 Selman A. Kurt, Kevin Albert Kasper, Qinghao Xu, Xin Yang, Xiao Xiao, Qihang Zeng 等14位作者
Nature Electronics, Published online: 10 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41928-026-01597-1 A sensor network that is wirelessly interconnected through a dual-mode metamaterial integrated into clothing can continuously monitor systolic blood pressure in dynamic environments such as during exercise.

item.title: Amorphous Tungsten Oxide Homojunction‐Based Optoelectronic Memristor Array Mimicking Local Image Enhancement for Network Intrusion Detection System
item.description:
📚 Wiley
👤 Wenhao Yang, Zepeng Li, Hao Kan, Yiming Wang, Qikai Guo, Yang Li
Advanced Functional Materials, Volume 36, Issue 29, 9 April 2026.

item.title: 基于流形学习与聚类的共格TPMS几何特征分类
item.description:
📚 ScienceDirect
该工作发展了一套针对三重周期性极小曲面（TPMS）的分类方法，结合流形学习降维与聚类算法提取几何特征。实现共格TPMS结构的系统归类，为多孔材料、仿生结构的设计与筛选提供高效工具，详情需查阅原文。
